meaning: adj.防守不住的,站不住脚的,不能维持的,支持不住的
examples: This argument is untenable from an intellectual, moral and practical standpoint.
That is clearly an untenable view, since editors can often intensify reader interest in a news topic by giving it frequent coverage.
Once man came to be seen as developing in the process of evolution, the idea of a substance which is contained in his essence seemed untenable.
Your escape route is clearly defined: Sell those municipal bonds whose headline risk is untenable.
Fairness norms may make paying some employees less than others untenable in the workplace.
